Bookreader Item Preview
Share or Embed This Item
texts
Mr. Pareto: Paid Political Content (Grey Propaganda)
- Topics
- Chavismo, Banana Republic, United Fruit, New Deal, Fiat Currency, Gold Standard, National Socialism, National Security, Keynesian Economics, Austerity (GATT - WTO), Neoliberalism, National Socialism, Privatization (Chile), Autogolpe, 11 September 2001, Behavior Under Trance (BUT), Anti-social Behavior Under Hypnosis, Memory Schema, Superego, Object Relations, Social-relational Aggression (TNA), Thematic Apperception, Stressful-dyad, National Training Laboratories (NTL), Large Group Awareness Training (LGAT), Positive Psychology (gestalt), Aggressive Idleness (Arbeitskraft), Media Arts, Class Antagonism, Psychological Strategy Board (Eisenhower)
- Collection
- magazine_clippings; magazine_rack
- Language
- English
- Item Size
- 956.3K
From now on we are paying everyone in bananas, until the National Debt is paid off. You're welcome.
(Mr. Pareto does not appear to be from the United States, or an American - by any means. The United States does have an unfortunate history however, of Nazi spies infiltrating social institutions shortly before world wars.)
----
Note:
'The United States went off the gold standard in two stages. The first significant step was during the Great Depression in 1933 when President Franklin D. Roosevelt signed Executive Order 6102, making it illegal for private individuals to hoard gold. This was part of the New Deal policies.
The complete abandonment of the gold standard, however, occurred later during the Nixon administration. In 1971, President Richard Nixon officially ended the convertibility of the U.S. dollar to gold, marking the shift to a fiat currency system.'
The complete abandonment of the gold standard, however, occurred later during the Nixon administration. In 1971, President Richard Nixon officially ended the convertibility of the U.S. dollar to gold, marking the shift to a fiat currency system.'
----
This comment was found (re-posted) on the Reddit platform, 3 January 2024.
Archived content similar to this, from 4chan (neoteny) is available; for research purposes.
----
Under Secretary of State for Public Diplomacy and Public Affairs (United States propaganda):
(PUBLIC LAW 105–277—OCT. 21, 1998; 112 STAT. 2681)
"Section 1(b) of the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 (22 U.S.C. 2651a(b)), as amended by this division, is further amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph: ‘‘(3) UNDER SECRETARY FOR PUBLIC DIPLOMACY.—There shall be in the Department of State, among the Under Secretaries authorized by paragraph (1), an Under Secretary for Public Diplomacy, who shall have primary responsibility to assist the Secretary and the Deputy Secretary in the formation and implementation of United States public diplomacy policies and activities, including international educational and cultural exchange programs, information, and international broadcasting.’’"
----
H.R.5736 - Smith-Mundt Modernization Act of 2012:
"Smith-Mundt Modernization Act of 2012 - Amends the United States Information and Educational Exchange Act of 1948 to authorize the Secretary of State and the Broadcasting Board of Governors to provide for the preparation and dissemination of information intended for foreign audiences abroad about the United States, including about its people, its history, and the federal government's policies, through press, publications, radio, motion pictures, the Internet, and other information media, including social media, and through information centers and instructors. (Under current law such authority is restricted to information disseminated abroad, with a limited domestic exception.)"
----
U.S. Commission on National Security / 21st Century (Hart-Rudman Commission):
Thomas Kean Sr., Principal author of the 9|11 Commission Report, is the descendant of Frank Gray Griswold, Ancestor of Gordon Gray, founder of CIA; slave and opium trade family, for East India Company (RJR Tobacco, and Banking.)

Downs, J. M. (1968). American Merchants and the China Opium Trade, 1800–1840. Business History Review, 42(04), 418–442. doi:10.2307/3112527
{'Frank Gray Griswold, A member of the prominent Griswold Family, his family's wealth originated from the China trade as his father and grandfather were merchants with 'N. L. and G. Griswold', which owned clipper ships that imported tea and silks from East Asia. Griswold was a financier. A friend and cousin of Pierre Lorillard, he served as a director and important executive of the Lorillard Tobacco Company from 1879 to 1893.'}
